title: The Forgotten Asylum
setting: A decaying early‑20th‑century mental institution haunted by the ghosts of its patients and a malevolent doctor.

characters.name: The Traveler
characters.description: A lost soul who stumbles upon the asylum, drawn into its unresolved trauma.

items.name: Rusted Key
items.description: A heavy, corroded key that looks like it hasn’t been used in decades.

items.name: Broken Fuse
items.description: A glass fuse with a blackened filament. It’s clearly blown.

items.name: Patient Chart
items.description: A faded medical file for a “Subject 7”. The notes are erratic, almost frantic.

items.name: Syringe
items.description: A sterile syringe, still sealed. The liquid inside glows faintly.

puzzles.result: The key turns with a gritty screech. The padlock falls apart, and the iron gate groans open.
You replace the blown fuse. The fluorescent lights flicker to life, illuminating a previously hidden panel beside the elevator.
The chart contains a code scrawled in the margins: 7‑3‑9. The door’s electronic lock beeps and clicks open.
The heavy key fits the reinforced basement door. It swings open, revealing a steep staircase descending into darkness.
You plunge the syringe into the portrait’s heart. The canvas ripples, and the ghost of Dr. Voss materializes, writhing in pain. The wall behind the portrait cracks open, revealing the isolation cell.

scenes.name: The Front Gate
scenes.prose: The iron gate is a twisted mouth of rust and decay. Weeds claw through cracked pavement, reaching for your ankles. A faded sign—HARWOOD SANATORIUM—hangs crooked, its letters barely legible. The sky is a bruised purple twilight, heavy with unshed rain. Shadows move in the upper‑floor windows, slow and deliberate. A low, rhythmic scratching comes from behind the gate, like something digging at the other side. The air tastes of damp earth and something sharper—ozone, maybe, or old blood. You are not welcome here, but the gate is shut, and the only way forward is through.

lookDescriptions.iron-gate: A heavy gate of rusted iron, twisted and shut. A padlock hangs from a chain.
lookDescriptions.loose-brick: A single brick is loose in the wall beside the gate. Something metallic glints behind it.
lookDescriptions.faded-sign: The sign reads 'HARWOOD SANATORIUM – EST. 1923'. The paint is peeling like dead skin.

scenes.name: The Main Reception
scenes.prose: The reception hall is a cathedral of collapse. A massive front desk has imploded, scattering splinters and dust. File cabinets lie overturned, their contents—endless patient records—spilled across the floor like leaves. A grand staircase leads upward, but the steps are rotten, unsafe. Flickering fluorescent tubes cast a sickly green light, buzzing like angry insects. The air is cold, unnaturally still. Papers rustle without a breeze. A single, persistent cold spot hangs in the center of the room, a pocket of winter in the damp chill. Something watches from the shadows behind the desk. You can feel its gaze.

lookDescriptions.collapsed-desk: The front desk has imploded, scattering wood and paper across the floor.
lookDescriptions.fuse-box: A metal box on the wall, its door hanging open. One fuse is missing.
lookDescriptions.grand-staircase: The steps are rotten and unsafe. They lead upward into darkness.
lookDescriptions.elevator: A brass elevator door, its call button broken.

scenes.name: The Patient Ward
scenes.prose: The ward is a long, narrow throat of rusted iron beds. Each frame is stripped bare, restraints dangling like dead vines. The air smells of mildew and antiseptic, a cloying combination that sticks in your throat. At the far end, a solitary window looks out onto a dead garden—brown stalks, grey soil. Whispers echo when you stand still, too faint to make out words. One of the beds is different: the sheets are rumpled, still warm, as if someone just rose. A heavy key hangs from a hook on the wall, glinting in the weak light. The whispers grow louder.

lookDescriptions.iron-beds: Rusted bedframes, each with leather restraints dangling like dead vines.
lookDescriptions.bedside-table: A small table beside one bed. A faded patient chart lies on top.
lookDescriptions.hook-on-wall: A heavy iron key hangs from a hook, labeled 'BASEMENT'.
lookDescriptions.dead-garden: Through the window, a garden of brown stalks and grey soil.

scenes.name: The Doctor’s Office
scenes.prose: The office is a preserved relic of polished wood and gleaming glass. A large desk faces a leather examination chair, its straps worn soft. Glass cabinets hold rows of medical instruments—scalpels, syringes, bone saws—all spotless under a single desk lamp that shouldn’t have power. A syringe lies on a metal tray, its liquid glowing faintly. On the wall, a portrait of Dr. Voss stares down, his eyes cold and analytical. The portrait’s gaze follows you. The examination chair stirs, its straps shifting as if recently used. The air is thick with the smell of polished leather and something chemical, sweet and wrong.

lookDescriptions.examination-chair: A leather chair with worn straps. It stirs as if recently occupied.
lookDescriptions.portrait: A painting of Dr. Voss. His eyes seem to follow you.
lookDescriptions.metal-tray: A sterile tray holding a syringe filled with glowing liquid.
lookDescriptions.glass-cabinets: Cabinets filled with medical instruments, all spotless.

scenes.name: The Basement Morgue
scenes.prose: Cold seeps into your bones. The morgue is a stainless‑steel tomb, lined with autopsy tables, each fitted with a drain in the floor. The air reeks of formaldehyde, thick enough to taste. A wall of refrigerated body drawers stands open, empty except for one at the far end—it hums violently, vibrating on its rails. Something inside scratches at the metal, a slow, insistent rhythm. The drains gurgle, belching thick, dark fluid that pools around your feet. This is where the asylum’s failures were taken apart, studied, discarded. The humming drawer grows louder. You are not alone down here.

lookDescriptions.autopsy-tables: Stainless‑steel tables, each with a drain. They are cold to the touch.
lookDescriptions.body-drawers: A wall of refrigerated drawers. One hums violently, vibrating on its rails.
lookDescriptions.clipboard: A clipboard with autopsy notes, the handwriting frantic and illegible.
lookDescriptions.drains: The floor drains gurgle, emitting a thick, dark fluid.

scenes.name: The Isolation Cell
scenes.prose: A padded room, walls stained with the ghosts of handprints. A single bare bulb swings from the ceiling, casting swinging shadows that dance across the stained padding. In the center of the room stands Dr. Voss, his white coat smeared with old blood, his face a mask of calm fury. He is not quite solid—a ghost, a memory given weight. The air is thick with the smell of ozone and decay. He raises a skeletal hand, pointing a finger at you. There are no exits. The door has locked behind you. This is where it ends, one way or another.

lookDescriptions.padded-walls: The walls are stained with handprints, old and faded.
lookDescriptions.swinging-bulb: A single bare bulb swings from the ceiling, casting dancing shadows.
lookDescriptions.dr-voss: The ghost of Dr. Voss stands in the center, his white coat stained, his gaze fixed on you.

winCondition.description: Dr. Voss’s ghost dissolves into mist, his final scream echoing through the asylum. The building groans as if released from a long‑held breath. You are free to leave, but the memories of this place will haunt you forever.
